entities.name: PHOSPHATE
entities.description: A chemical nutrient whose adsorption and availability are studied in different soil types.

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: A type of soil characterized by high clay content and specific phosphate sorption properties.

entities.name: ALFISOL
entities.description: A type of soil compared with Vertisol regarding phosphate sorption and availability.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID ZONE OF INDIA
entities.description: The geographic region where the studied soils are located.

entities.name: ICRISAT CENTRE
entities.description: The specific research facility where the soil samples were obtained.

entities.name: ADSORPTION ISOTHERMS
entities.description: A method used to measure total adsorbed and isotopically exchangeable phosphate.

entities.name: FREUNDLICH ISOTHERM
entities.description: A mathematical model used to describe the relationship between exchangeable P and total adsorbed P with phosphate solution concentration.

entities.name: PHOSPHATE SOLUTION CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The concentration of phosphate in the solution used to determine adsorption relationships.

entities.name: FERTILIZER P
entities.description: Phosphorus added to the soil to improve crop yield.

entities.name: 32P
entities.description: A radioactive isotope of phosphorus used to label phosphate for exchangeability studies.

entities.name: CACL2 EXTRACTION
entities.description: A chemical extraction method used to measure available phosphorus in the soil.

entities.name: YIELD RESPONSE
entities.description: The growth or production response of crops to the application of phosphorus.

entities.name: CROPS
entities.description: Plants grown in Vertisols and Alfisols that utilize available phosphate.
